title: barringtonia ceylanica
intro: Barringtonia ceylanica, also known as the fish-poison tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae, native to Sri Lanka and other parts of South Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Barringtonia ceylanica include its ability to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions.
